Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Met releases high-def 3D scans of 140 famous art objects 316 upvotes, 63 comments
  2. Show HN: Algorithms and Data Structures in TypeScript – Free Book (~400 Pages) 52 upvotes, 6 comments
  3. Understanding the Go Runtime: The Scheduler 140 upvotes, 24 comments
  4. Vite+ Alpha: Unified Toolchain for the Web (MIT License) 34 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Okmain: How to pick an OK main colour of an image 117 upvotes, 22 comments
  6. IMG_0416 (2024) 148 upvotes, 32 comments
  7. What we learned from a 22-Day storage bug (and how we fixed it) 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. Dijkstra's Crisis: The End of Algol and Beginning of Software Engineering (2010) [pdf] 22 upvotes, 2 comments
  9. Run NanoClaw in Docker Sandboxes 54 upvotes, 10 comments
  10. Vite 8.0 Is Out 435 upvotes, 135 comments
  11. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 364 upvotes, 111 comments
  12. Ceno, browse the web without internet access 91 upvotes, 23 comments
  13. Willingness to look stupid 534 upvotes, 180 comments
  14. Shall I implement it? No 1422 upvotes, 514 comments
  15. I traced $2B in grants and 45 states' lobbying behind age‑verification bills 642 upvotes, 227 comments
  16. Executing programs inside transformers with exponentially faster inference 195 upvotes, 61 comments
  17. Gvisor on Raspbian 25 upvotes, 4 comments
  18. “This is not the computer for you” 705 upvotes, 281 comments
  19. E2E encrypted messaging on Instagram will no longer be supported after 8 May 88 upvotes, 27 comments
  20. Enhancing gut-brain communication reversed cognitive decline in aging mice 350 upvotes, 167 comments
  21. Show HN: What was the world listening to? Music charts, 20 countries (1940–2025) 57 upvotes, 20 comments
  22. Prompt-caching – auto-injects Anthropic cache breakpoints (90% token savings) 61 upvotes, 24 comments
  23. Bucketsquatting is (finally) dead 208 upvotes, 107 comments
  24. TUI Studio – visual terminal UI design tool 224 upvotes, 121 comments
  25. An old photo of a large BBS (2022) 242 upvotes, 152 comments
  26. Nanny state discovers Linux, demands it check kids' IDs before booting 134 upvotes, 99 comments
  27. Monster Is the Machine 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. Source code of Swedish e-government services has been leaked 135 upvotes, 123 comments
  29. ATMs didn’t kill bank teller jobs, but the iPhone did 472 upvotes, 488 comments
  30. Launch HN: Spine Swarm (YC S23) – AI agents that collaborate on a visual canvas 25 upvotes, 20 comments

Last generated 11:00pm, 13 March 2026. Source here.
